title: Resolve Review P2: Surface transcript snapshot persistence failures
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The app silently ignores errors when it tries to refresh `transcript.json` during recording. If those writes fail, the UI still looks healthy even though the persisted session snapshot is now stale.

## Current Behavior
The live transcript snapshot handler uses `try?` when calling `sessionRepository.updateTranscriptSnapshot(...)` ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:711](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)). That means disk-write failures disappear with no status change, no degraded state, and no manifest marker.

## Why It Matters
Phase 1 depends on preserving the committed transcript timeline for incomplete-session recovery. Silent persistence failures undermine that contract and make recovery bugs much harder to diagnose.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
Disk-full or write-denied occurs mid-recording. The operator keeps seeing transcript chunks in the UI, but `transcript.json` stops updating. If the app then exits unexpectedly, the recovered incomplete session opens with an older transcript snapshot than the one the operator saw.

## Evidence
- Silent write: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:711](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Repository update path: [MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/SessionRepository.swift:124](./MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/SessionRepository.swift)

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Handle snapshot-write failures explicitly. Surface them in the recording state, mark persistence as degraded or incomplete, and make sure the manifest preserves that failure so recovery behavior stays honest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Transcript snapshot write failures are visible in recording state and logs.
- A failed snapshot write leaves an honest degraded/incomplete marker in persisted metadata.
- Reviewers can inject a repository write failure and see bounded, user-visible handling.

title: Resolve Review P1: Freeze the saved transcript at the user-visible Stop snapshot
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
After the user presses Stop, the app currently flushes more transcript windows and can save text that was never visible before the stop action. That conflicts with the locked Phase 1 contract.

## Current Behavior
`stopShellSession()` stops capture first, then calls `finishPendingWork()` ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:740](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)). `finishPendingWork()` explicitly requests flushes on every source lane and keeps scheduling additional transcript windows until buffers are empty ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:239](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)).

## Why It Matters
The contract says v1 does not do a final post-recording transcription pass (`D12`) and that the saved transcript snapshot must match what the operator saw during recording (`D23`). The current stop behavior can rewrite that snapshot after capture has already ended.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
The operator presses Stop while there are still buffered frames waiting to transcribe. The app spends extra time processing those windows after stop, then persists extra chunks into `transcript.json`. The reopened session now shows transcript text the operator never saw on-screen before stopping.

## Evidence
- Stop path: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:740](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Flush behavior: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:239](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Locked decisions: [history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md:24](./history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md), [history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md:30](./history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md)

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Treat the last visible `currentTranscriptChunks()` snapshot as authoritative when Stop is pressed. Do not schedule new transcript work after stop; at most allow already in-flight work to settle if that does not change the persisted contract, otherwise freeze immediately and mark leftover audio as untranslated backlog.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pressing Stop does not create new committed transcript chunks that were not already visible.
- `transcript.json` matches the user-visible timeline at the moment of Stop.
- The stop path remains consistent with `D12` and `D23`.
close_reason: Changed Stop to persist the frozen on-screen transcript snapshot instead of flushing new post-stop windows, keeping the saved transcript aligned with the user-visible recording timeline.

title: Resolve Review P3: Redact artifact paths from public logs
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The app currently logs full artifact directory paths as public values. That exposes user home-directory details and per-session identifiers in unified logs.

## Current Behavior
Both the capture layer and coordinator log artifact directory paths with `privacy: .public` ([MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift:133](./MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:700](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)).

## Why It Matters
This is not a functional blocker, but it leaks more local storage detail than support logs need for debugging.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
A shared diagnostic log bundle includes the full local session path, exposing the username/home path and session identifier for recorded meetings.

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Log only a short session identifier or mark full paths as private.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public logs no longer contain full artifact directory paths.
- Support logs still retain enough non-sensitive identifiers for debugging.

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can the session bundle preserve incomplete recordings and the exact live transcript snapshot without overengineering storage?
description: Validation spike for session persistence and incomplete recovery.
close_reason: YES: A file-backed session bundle can preserve incomplete recordings and the exact committed live transcript snapshot without needing a database in Phase 1.

title: Resolve Review P1: Make whisper bootstrap reproducible on a clean checkout
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The app currently builds only if a locally generated `Vendor/whisper.cpp/build-apple/whisper.xcframework` already exists. That framework is not tracked in git, so a clean checkout cannot build Phase 1 at all.

## Current Behavior
The Xcode project references `Vendor/whisper.cpp/build-apple/whisper.xcframework` directly ([Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:54](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj)). Locally, that path exists because the nested vendor repo has generated artifacts, but `git ls-files -s Vendor/whisper.cpp` shows the path is a gitlink and a clean local clone produced an empty `Vendor/whisper.cpp` directory with no `build-apple` output.

## Why It Matters
This breaks the Phase 1 build reproducibility promise around the whisper packaging boundary. A teammate, CI job, or fresh machine cannot even compile the app without manual local state that is not encoded in the repo.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
A fresh clone at `/tmp/meetless-review-yzY69Q/clean` failed with `There is no XCFramework found at ... Vendor/whisper.cpp/build-apple/whisper.xcframework` when running `xcodebuild -project Meetless.xcodeproj -scheme Meetless -configuration Debug -sdk macosx build`.

## Evidence
- Project reference: [Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:54](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj)
- Local bootstrap helper: [scripts/build-whisper-xcframework.sh:1](./scripts/build-whisper-xcframework.sh)
- Approach expects bootstrap/setup to make this reproducible: [history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/approach.md:159](./history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/approach.md)

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Make the framework bootstrap explicit and reproducible from the repo itself. That can be a tracked setup path that initializes the vendor dependency and builds `whisper.xcframework`, or a committed/prebuilt artifact strategy, but the project must not rely on untracked local state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A clean checkout can build `Meetless` after following one documented bootstrap path.
- The repo encodes how `Vendor/whisper.cpp` is obtained and how `whisper.xcframework` is produced.
- Reviewer can repeat the clean-checkout build without depending on pre-existing local artifacts.
close_reason: Added a tracked whisper bootstrap path via .gitmodules plus scripts/bootstrap-whisper.sh, and verified a clean checkout can bootstrap whisper.xcframework and build Meetless successfully.

title: Phase 2 / Story 1: Read local session bundles into history state
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 2 - Saved Sessions Feel Like a Product
What Changes: Saved local session bundles become a real browseable history list instead of artifacts that only exist on disk.
Unlocks Next: Session selection and detail rendering can rely on a real saved-session list.

## Story Context

Story: Story 1 - History list from local sessions
What Happens: Add the read-side repository and history wiring that turns saved bundles into real rows.
Contributes To: The Phase 2 exit state requiring real saved sessions to appear in history with the locked row contract.
Unlocks: Story 2 can open a selected session into detail.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Keep file IO in SessionRepository, add public summary DTOs, and let the existing app shell own refresh/navigation state instead of introducing a new persistence layer.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/History/*
- MeetlessApp/App/* where saved-session refresh or selection state is introduced

## Task

- Extend SessionRepository with public read-side models and list APIs for saved sessions.
- Decode the existing session bundle contract instead of redefining storage.
- Wire history loading and refresh into the current app shell.
- Render real history rows that stay browse-only and show title, date/time, duration, and transcript preview.
- Make incomplete sessions visibly distinct in the list.

## Verification Criteria

- Relaunching the app after a Phase 1 recording shows real saved sessions in history.
- The history list is sorted predictably and uses the locked row contract.
- Incomplete sessions are visibly marked instead of blending into completed sessions.
- The implementation reads the existing bundle contract rather than inventing a second storage path.

## Acceptance Criteria

- SessionRepository exposes a public saved-session summary surface for the history screen.
- The history screen renders real saved sessions from local bundles.
- History remains browse-only with no search or filters.
- Incomplete sessions are clearly visible in the list.
close_reason: Completed: read-side session summaries now hydrate browse-only history rows from local bundles

title: Resolve Review P2: Surface transcript-lane degradation when retry-exhausted windows are dropped
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The new stop path correctly freezes the visible transcript snapshot, but it can now give up on a transcript window after three failures without surfacing that loss as a degraded-state warning. That means the app can silently save a partial transcript lane even though the user never gets told one source became unreliable.

## Current Behavior
Inside `TranscriptCoordinator.handleProcessingOutcome(...)`, a failed window is retried up to three times and then dropped by calling `lane.markTranscriptionFinished()` instead of restoring it again ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:352](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)). `stopShellSession()` then freezes and persists whatever committed chunks were visible at stop time ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:771](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)).

## Why It Matters
This is a good tradeoff for bounded Stop behavior, but it still leaves one honesty gap: the user-visible session state does not clearly say that one transcript lane degraded and that some chunks were dropped. Decision `D15` in practice means the app should keep the surviving work alive and show a clear degraded-state warning when one lane stops being trustworthy. Decision `D23` means the saved snapshot should match what was visible, but it should still be honest about missing coverage.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
The microphone lane hits a repeated whisper decode error for frames `12000-15000`. After the third failure, that window is dropped so Stop can finish. The session saves the exact visible transcript snapshot, but the operator never sees a warning that the Me transcript lane degraded and lost coverage for part of the meeting.

## Evidence
- Retry-exhausted drop path: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:352](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Frozen save path: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:771](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Locked decisions: [history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md:16](./history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md), [history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md:30](./history/native-macos-meeting-recorder/CONTEXT.md)

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
When a transcript window is dropped permanently, mark that source lane as degraded in the recording/session state and surface a clear warning in the UI and final saved-session metadata. The stop path can stay bounded, but the app should be explicit that the transcript is now partial for that source.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A retry-exhausted transcript window marks its source lane as degraded instead of only logging.
- The recording UI and final session state clearly tell the user that transcript coverage became partial for that source.
- The bounded Stop behavior remains intact while the saved transcript snapshot stays aligned with `D23`.

title: Phase 1 / Story 2: Normalize per-source audio, file writing, and degraded-state handling
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: Each captured source becomes a durable, transcription-ready pipeline instead of raw callback traffic.
Unlocks Next: Live transcript coordination and session persistence can trust the source streams and health signals.

## Story Context

Story: Story 2 - Dual-source recording pipeline
What Happens: Convert each source into normalized PCM, write durable per-source audio files, and keep degraded-state truth explicit when one input fails.
Contributes To: The phase exit state requiring separate source pipelines and incomplete-session survivability.
Unlocks: Transcript merging and saved session bundle finalization.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Keep system audio mapped to Meeting and microphone mapped to Me, normalize both into transcription-ready PCM, write one durable audio file per source, and continue honestly if one source fails.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/AudioPipeline/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/* for source health display hooks

## Task

- Create per-source normalization pipelines from capture buffers into the internal PCM shape.
- Add durable file writing for each source artifact.
- Track source health, degradation, and interruption signals.
- Ensure the session can continue with the surviving source when one source fails.

## Verification Criteria

- Meeting and Me each produce independent transcription-ready PCM output.
- Durable audio files are written per source during the session.
- Source degradation is detectable and does not automatically discard the entire session.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Meeting and Me each produce independent transcription-ready PCM output.
- Durable per-source audio files are written during the session.
- One source can degrade without forcing the entire session to fail.
acceptance_criteria: Meeting and Me each produce independent transcription-ready PCM output. | Durable per-source audio files are written during the session. | One source can degrade without forcing the entire session to fail.
notes: Validated by spike bd-2o8 and bd-2ne: assume one ScreenCaptureKit session emits separate Meeting and Me streams, keep per-source health explicit, and preserve a surviving source when the other fails.
close_reason: Implemented per-source PCM normalization, durable WAV writing, degraded-state signaling, and verified with xcodebuild -project Meetless.xcodeproj -scheme Meetless -configuration Debug build.

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can whisper.cpp be packaged behind an XCFramework wrapper that stays isolated from the app target?
description: Validation spike for whisper.cpp packaging boundary.
close_reason: YES: whisper.cpp supports an XCFramework-based wrapper path that can stay isolated from the main app target on the current local Apple toolchain.

title: Phase 1 / Story 1: Isolate whisper bridge and bundled-model smoke transcription
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: The app gains a safe local speech-to-text boundary that can be called from Swift code without leaking native build complexity through the whole target.
Unlocks Next: Real recording pipelines can depend on a proven transcription engine seam.

## Story Context

Story: Story 1 - App shell and native transcription boundary
What Happens: Add the isolated whisper.cpp wrapper/package boundary, model loading path, and one in-app smoke transcription path.
Contributes To: The phase exit state requiring live local transcript capability.
Unlocks: Dual-source recording can feed transcription workers instead of a speculative API.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Do not compile whisper.cpp directly into the main app target. Wrap a pinned whisper.xcframework behind a Swift-facing bridge and keep one actor-isolated worker per source.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- Vendor/whisper.cpp/*
- MeetlessPackages/WhisperCppBridge/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/*
- MeetlessApp/Resources/Models/*
- bootstrap/build scripts needed for local framework assembly

## Task

- Introduce the pinned native wrapper boundary for whisper.cpp.
- Implement Swift-facing load/transcribe lifecycle APIs appropriate for source workers.
- Bundle a default Phase 1 model from app resources, starting with a modest class such as `base.en` or smaller/quantized if early validation says memory pressure is too high.
- Keep one isolated context per source worker rather than sharing a mutable whisper context across both sources.
- Prove the bridge inside the app environment with a smoke transcription path that validating can exercise.

## Verification Criteria

- The app can load the bundled model through the wrapper without app-wide native flag leakage.
- A small local smoke transcription path returns text through the Swift-facing API.
- The resulting bridge contract is suitable for one worker per source in later beads.
- The chosen default model is packaged from app resources and stays within the modest Phase 1 constraint.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The isolated whisper wrapper loads the bundled model from the app environment.
- A smoke transcription path returns text through the Swift-facing bridge.
- The bridge contract is usable by one worker per source without leaking vendor build settings into the app target.
- The default bundled model stays within the modest Phase 1 sizing strategy.
acceptance_criteria: The isolated whisper wrapper loads the bundled model from the app environment. | A smoke transcription path returns text through the Swift-facing bridge. | The bridge contract is usable by one worker per source without leaking vendor build settings into the app target.
notes: Validated by spike bd-2gx and bd-2ne: keep whisper.cpp behind an XCFramework-backed wrapper boundary, load the model from app resources, and use one isolated context per source instead of sharing a mutable context.
close_reason: Completed: isolated whisper bridge and bundled in-app smoke transcription path

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can one whisper context per source keep live dual-source transcription believable on Apple Silicon?
description: Validation spike for live dual-source transcription workers.
close_reason: YES: One isolated whisper context per source is the correct concurrency model for Phase 1, provided the model class stays modest and Core ML remains optional.

title: Spike: Phase 1 - Can one ScreenCaptureKit session provide stable Meeting and Me source streams for whole-system audio plus microphone?
description: Validation spike for ScreenCaptureKit dual-source capture topology.
close_reason: YES: One ScreenCaptureKit session can provide separate .audio and .microphone outputs on the planned macOS 15+ baseline, with permission repair and relaunch messaging handled in the app flow.

title: Phase 2 / Story 2: Open saved sessions into transcript-plus-metadata detail
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 2 - Saved Sessions Feel Like a Product
What Changes: Saved sessions stop at the list stage and become real transcript-plus-metadata destinations.
Unlocks Next: Delete and honesty presentation can attach to one selected-session model instead of placeholder screens.

## Story Context

Story: Story 2 - Session detail from persisted snapshot
What Happens: Add selected-session state plus detail loading so a saved session opens into the exact persisted snapshot and metadata.
Contributes To: The Phase 2 exit state requiring a real read-only detail surface.
Unlocks: Story 3 can delete and present honest saved-session state from a real selected session.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Put selected-session state in AppModel, keep detail read-only, and load transcript plus metadata through repository DTOs rather than parsing JSON inside the view.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/App/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/History/* where row selection is wired
- MeetlessApp/Features/SessionDetail/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/*

## Task

- Add app-level selected-session state.
- Load one saved session detail record from the existing session bundle.
- Render the persisted transcript snapshot and metadata in the Session Detail destination.
- Keep the detail screen read-only and omit playback/edit/export controls.

## Verification Criteria

- Selecting a history row opens the matching saved session into the detail destination.
- The detail screen renders the persisted transcript snapshot rather than placeholder text.
- The detail metadata includes the saved session state without introducing playback or transcript editing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The app can open a saved session from history into a real detail screen.
- Session Detail renders transcript plus metadata only.
- The transcript content shown in detail comes from the persisted snapshot contract established in Phase 1.
- No playback, editing, export, or sharing controls are introduced.
close_reason: Completed: wired saved-session detail loading and read-only transcript metadata view

title: Phase 1 / Story 3: Merge live transcript chunks into labeled recording UI
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: The running session becomes visible to the user as live Meeting/Me transcript output rather than hidden background processing.
Unlocks Next: The app can prove the core user-facing value and later save/reopen flows have a transcript timeline to persist.

## Story Context

Story: Story 3 - Live transcript and session save
What Happens: Connect source workers to a transcript coordinator and show committed, source-labeled transcript chunks in the recording UI.
Contributes To: The phase exit state requiring live Meeting and Me transcript chunks during recording.
Unlocks: Session snapshot persistence and Phase 2 saved-session browsing.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Use committed chunk-level updates, not unstable token streaming, and merge source-local emissions into one ordered timeline labeled Meeting and Me.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/Transcription/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/TranscriptCoordinator/*
- MeetlessApp/App/* if shared state needs routing

## Task

- Define the transcript chunk model for Meeting and Me.
- Wire one worker per source into a transcript coordinator.
- Update the recording view model and UI with committed live chunks and current status.
- Keep the transcript timeline stable enough to persist exactly as seen.
- Keep Phase 1 aligned with `D12` by using the live timeline as the final saved transcript, not a separate post-recording pass.

## Verification Criteria

- During recording, the UI shows labeled chunks for both sources with believable live delay.
- Transcript events remain ordered and attributable to the correct source.
- The transcript timeline is in a form that can be snapshotted directly for persistence.
- The design does not rely on a final retranscription pass after Stop.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The recording UI shows committed live transcript chunks labeled Meeting and Me.
- Transcript events remain ordered and attributable to the correct source.
- The transcript timeline can be snapshotted directly for persistence.
- The Phase 1 transcript flow does not introduce a final retranscription pass.
acceptance_criteria: The recording UI shows committed live transcript chunks labeled Meeting and Me. | Transcript events remain ordered and attributable to the correct source. | The transcript timeline can be snapshotted directly for persistence.
notes: Validated by spike bd-2ne and bd-1yz: consume committed chunk updates from source-local workers, keep the live timeline as the canonical transcript shape, and do not add a separate post-recording transcription pass in Phase 1.

title: Resolve Review P2: Re-enable code signing so sandbox entitlements actually apply
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The project enables App Sandbox in build settings but disables code signing in both Debug and Release. Unsigned macOS apps do not get those entitlements enforced, so the current recording data path behaves like unsandboxed local storage.

## Current Behavior
The target build settings set `ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX = YES` and `CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED = NO` in both configurations ([Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:398](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj), [Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:403](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj), [Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:545](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj), [Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:550](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj)). Session artifacts are then written under user Application Support ([MeetlessApp/Services/AudioPipeline/SourceAudioPipeline.swift:24](./MeetlessApp/Services/AudioPipeline/SourceAudioPipeline.swift)).

## Why It Matters
Meetless stores raw meeting audio plus transcript snapshots. If sandboxing is part of the privacy story, the current build settings do not actually enforce it.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
A background agent running as the same user account watches `~/Library/Application Support/Meetless/Sessions/*` and copies `meeting.wav`, `me.wav`, and transcript snapshots because the app is not running inside an enforced sandbox container.

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Re-enable code signing for the app target, add or verify the entitlements file, and confirm the runtime storage path and permissions match the intended sandboxed behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Debug and Release builds use code signing when sandbox is enabled.
- The app has an explicit entitlements configuration that reviewers can inspect.
- Storage behavior is verified under the intended sandbox/container model.

title: Phase 1 / Story 2: Orchestrate ScreenCaptureKit session and permission gate
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: One recording session can start from the app shell and either enter a real capture run or block with a focused repair path.
Unlocks Next: Per-source pipelines and live transcript work can attach to real session lifecycle events.

## Story Context

Story: Story 2 - Dual-source recording pipeline
What Happens: Build the recording coordinator, permission readiness checks, and ScreenCaptureKit session orchestration for system audio plus microphone.
Contributes To: The phase exit state requiring startable recording with separate source capture.
Unlocks: Per-source normalization, file writing, and degraded-state tracking.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Use one ScreenCaptureKit-driven capture service with separate output handling for .audio and .microphone, and treat Screen Recording and microphone permissions as distinct repair cases.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/Permissions/*
- MeetlessApp/App/* where coordinator wiring is needed

## Task

- Implement the permission gate for screen recording and microphone access.
- Add the recording coordinator lifecycle for Start/Stop.
- Configure ScreenCaptureKit for whole-system audio plus microphone capture.
- Surface blocked and ready states cleanly to the recording flow.
- Make the blocked repair path honor `D20` and `D21` by deep-linking to the relevant System Settings destination, keeping guidance short, and calling out any relaunch requirement.

## Verification Criteria

- Pressing Record performs the expected readiness checks.
- Missing permissions produce a focused repair path instead of a silent failure.
- The repair path opens the correct System Settings destination and explains any relaunch requirement.
- A session can start ScreenCaptureKit capture with distinct audio and microphone outputs wired into downstream handlers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pressing Record checks Screen Recording and microphone readiness.
- Missing permissions surface a focused repair path rather than silent failure.
- The repair path deep-links to System Settings with short guidance and any relaunch note.
- A session can start ScreenCaptureKit capture with distinct audio and microphone outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing Record checks Screen Recording and microphone readiness. | Missing permissions surface a focused repair path rather than silent failure. | A session can start ScreenCaptureKit capture with distinct audio and microphone outputs.
notes: Validated by spike bd-2o8: target macOS 15+ single-framework ScreenCaptureKit capture, treat Screen Recording and microphone permission separately, and make the repair flow open System Settings with relaunch guidance when capture permission was just granted.
close_reason: Completed: wired ScreenCaptureKit start-stop coordination and focused permission repair flow

title: Native macOS Meeting Recorder
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Whole Feature
What Changes: Build a brand-new local-first native macOS app that records whole-system meeting audio plus microphone input, shows live labeled transcript, and saves sessions locally.
Unlocks Next: Phase 2 validation and later hardening work.

## Story Context

Story: Whole Feature
What Happens: The full feature is decomposed into an executable phase graph for Khuym delivery.
Contributes To: The complete product contract in CONTEXT.md.
Unlocks: Phase-specific planning, validation, and execution.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Use a single native macOS SwiftUI app target, isolate whisper.cpp behind a wrapper boundary, keep system audio and microphone separate end-to-end, and persist local file-backed session bundles.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## Scope

- Tracks all work for the native macOS meeting recorder feature.
- Parent for the completed Phase 1 graph and the prepared Phase 2 graph.
- Phase 3 hardening and review follow-ups remain attached under the same epic as they are prepared.

## Verification Criteria

- The active phase task graph is attached under this epic.
- Story and phase lineage stay traceable from planning artifacts to executable tasks.
- The current Khuym state points at the same epic and current-phase beads.

## Success Criteria

- Phase 1 and Phase 2 beads exist with explicit story and phase lineage.
- Khuym planning artifacts point to the same current-phase execution graph.
- The feature can advance into validating without missing planning context.

title: Resolve Review P1: Make Stop finalize bounded and fail-safe
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
The current stop path can fail to finish a recording cleanly. Two separate failure modes stack together: repeated transcription errors can retry forever, and an `SCStream.stopCapture()` error can abort cleanup before the session is finalized.

## Current Behavior
Inside `TranscriptCoordinator`, a failed window restores the same frame range and immediately reschedules it with no retry budget ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:334](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:346](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)). `finishPendingWork()` then waits until outstanding work disappears ([MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:239](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)). Separately, `ScreenCaptureSession.stop()` throws straight through `stream.stopCapture()` before the normal finalize/reset path runs ([MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift:150](./MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:740](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)).

## Why It Matters
Phase 1 is supposed to leave behind a saved session bundle even when things go wrong. Right now a bad transcription window or stop error can keep Stop spinning forever or short-circuit the incomplete-session recovery path.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
If the bundled model is missing or corrupt, every transcription window can fail. The lane keeps retrying the same window, `finishPendingWork()` never returns, and pressing Stop never completes. If ScreenCaptureKit also throws during `stopCapture()`, the coordinator exits before best-effort finalization can mark the session incomplete and clean up state.

## Evidence
- Infinite retry loop: [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:239](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:334](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:346](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)
- Throwing stop path: [MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift:150](./MeetlessApp/Services/Capture/ScreenCaptureSession.swift), [MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift:740](./MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/RecordingViewModel.swift)

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Make Stop best-effort and bounded. Add retry budgeting or a terminal degraded state for failed transcript windows, and ensure `ScreenCaptureSession.stop()` always runs cleanup/finalize logic with `defer`-style guarantees even when `stopCapture()` reports an error.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pressing Stop always returns within a bounded time.
- Transcription failures cannot loop forever on the same frame window.
- A `stopCapture()` failure still leaves the session finalized as incomplete and clears in-memory recording state.
close_reason: Made Stop bounded and best-effort by freezing transcript ingestion, capping per-window retries, dropping permanently failing windows, and allowing ScreenCaptureSession.stop() cleanup to continue after stopCapture() errors.

title: Resolve Review P2: Add automated regression coverage for the Phase 1 core loop
description:
## Plain-Language Summary
Phase 1 currently has no automated tests or test target. The only verified gate in this review was a manual `xcodebuild build`, which leaves the most fragile recording, transcript, and persistence paths uncovered.

## Current Behavior
`xcodebuild -list -project Meetless.xcodeproj` reports only one target and one scheme (`Meetless`). The project file does not define an XCTest bundle or `xcodebuild test` path, so regressions in transcript ordering, stop/finalize behavior, and persistence edge cases are all manual-only.

## Why It Matters
The current Phase 1 implementation now has several non-trivial state-machine paths. Without executable regression coverage, fixes for the blocking review issues can easily regress something adjacent.

## Concrete Failure Scenario
A future change tweaks transcript window scheduling to fix Stop behavior but accidentally reorders committed chunks or breaks incomplete-session finalization. The app still builds, but the regression ships because there is no test suite covering those invariants.

## Evidence
- Project lists only the app target and scheme: local `xcodebuild -list -project Meetless.xcodeproj` review run
- No test target defined in [Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj](./Meetless.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj)
- Traceability source epic: `bd-3cy`

## Smallest Credible Fix Direction
Add a minimal `MeetlessTests` target and wire `xcodebuild test` for the highest-value pure-Swift surfaces first: transcript coordination, session repository behavior, and recording state transitions around stop/incomplete handling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The repo defines at least one XCTest target and a working `xcodebuild test` command.
- Core Phase 1 invariants have focused automated coverage.
- Review fixes for the P1 stop/snapshot issues land with regression tests.

title: Phase 1 / Story 1: Scaffold native app shell and recording-first home flow
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: The app becomes a real launchable macOS product shell instead of only planning artifacts.
Unlocks Next: The native transcription boundary and recording pipeline can plug into a real app target.

## Story Context

Story: Story 1 - App shell and native transcription boundary
What Happens: Create the first native macOS app scaffold with a recording-first home screen and Start/Stop entry flow.
Contributes To: A launchable app target existing for the phase exit state.
Unlocks: The isolated whisper bridge and later recording coordinator work.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Keep the UI architecture lightweight with a single SwiftUI app target, simple navigation, and a home screen that emphasizes the record action.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- Meetless.xcodeproj
- MeetlessApp/App/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/Home/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/*
- MeetlessApp/Resources/*

## Task

- Scaffold the greenfield macOS app project.
- Create the initial app/window entrypoint and recording-first home experience.
- Define the minimal app model and navigation shells needed for later recording/history/detail screens without overbuilding Phase 2 UI.
- Expose the Start/Stop control surface and placeholder status regions that later beads can wire into real services.

## Verification Criteria

- The app builds and launches locally into a windowed SwiftUI shell.
- The home screen clearly exposes the record action and minimal recording status area.
- The structure leaves obvious attachment points for recording coordination and later Phase 2 navigation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The macOS app target builds and launches into a windowed SwiftUI shell.
- The home screen exposes a clear Start/Stop recording entry point.
- The shell leaves clean integration points for recording coordination and later navigation.
acceptance_criteria: The macOS app target builds and launches into a windowed SwiftUI shell. | The home screen exposes a clear Start/Stop recording entry point. | The shell leaves clean integration points for recording coordination and later navigation.
close_reason: Completed: scaffolded macOS app shell and recording-first home flow

title: Phase 2 / Story 3: Delete sessions and surface saved-session honesty
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 2 - Saved Sessions Feel Like a Product
What Changes: The saved-session flow becomes intentional and honest instead of passive file browsing.
Unlocks Next: Phase 3 can focus on hardening and release trust instead of missing product behavior.

## Story Context

Story: Story 3 - Delete and saved-session honesty presentation
What Happens: Add delete actions, keep incomplete sessions visibly distinct, and establish a concrete saved-session warning surface for honest status messaging.
Contributes To: The Phase 2 exit state requiring local delete and honest saved-session presentation.
Unlocks: Phase 3 hardening over a complete saved-session loop.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Delete should stay folder-based and local-only, and Phase 2 should create a warning presentation seam that later review follow-ups like bd-2ap and bd-15x can enrich without blocking this phase.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/App/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/History/*
- MeetlessApp/Features/SessionDetail/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/*

## Task

- Add local delete actions for saved sessions.
- Refresh list and selected-session state cleanly after removal.
- Keep incomplete sessions visibly marked in history/detail.
- Add a concrete saved-session warning presentation surface so the UI stays honest today and later review follow-ups can plug richer degraded-state markers into the same seam.

## Verification Criteria

- Deleting a session removes the local bundle and refreshes the visible list/detail state.
- Incomplete sessions remain clearly marked in the saved-session UI.
- The UI has a concrete saved-session warning surface that can show current saved-session status honestly without inventing unsupported certainty.
- The implementation stays local-only and does not add soft-delete, export, or cloud sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A user can delete a saved session from the Phase 2 UI and the local bundle is removed.
- Saved-session UI refreshes cleanly after deletion.
- Incomplete sessions remain visibly distinct.
- Phase 2 establishes the saved-session warning surface without depending on later review beads to close the phase.
close_reason: Completed: local delete flow and saved-session honesty presentation

title: Phase 1 / Story 3: Persist session bundles and incomplete-session recovery state
description:
## Phase Context

Phase: Phase 1 - First Live Recording Loop
What Changes: A recording no longer disappears when it ends or fails; it leaves behind a concrete local session bundle that later product phases can read.
Unlocks Next: Phase 2 history, detail, delete, and incomplete-session presentation work.

## Story Context

Story: Story 3 - Live transcript and session save
What Happens: Persist session metadata, transcript snapshot, and per-source audio artifacts on stop or interruption so unfinished sessions are incomplete rather than lost.
Contributes To: The phase exit state requiring a saved bundle and incomplete-session survivability.
Unlocks: Saved-session UX built on real local artifacts.

## Planning Context

From approach.md: Store each session as a self-contained bundle under Application Support with session.json, transcript.json, meeting.wav, and me.wav, and treat the live transcript timeline as the persisted snapshot for D23.

## Institutional Learnings

No prior learnings for this domain.

## File Scope

- MeetlessApp/Services/SessionRepository/*
- MeetlessApp/Services/Transcription/* where snapshot hooks are required
- MeetlessApp/Features/Recording/* for stop/failure finalization hooks
- local fixture/sample session paths if needed for validation

## Task

- Define the on-disk session bundle contract for Phase 1.
- Persist session metadata, transcript snapshot, and per-source audio artifacts.
- Persist enough metadata early in the session lifecycle that interrupted recordings can still reopen as incomplete.
- Snapshot the committed transcript timeline incrementally during recording or immediately after committed-chunk updates, not only at graceful Stop time.
- Finalize completed sessions on Stop and preserve partial sessions on interruption.
- Expose enough status and metadata that Phase 2 can read sessions without redefining the schema.
- Honor `D7`, `D11`, and `D23` explicitly by storing a timestamp-based default title, treating per-source raw audio as the durable source of record, and saving the exact live transcript snapshot without a later rewrite.

## Verification Criteria

- Stopping a session writes the expected local bundle structure.
- Session metadata includes a timestamp-based default title suitable for Phase 2 history rows.
- Interrupted sessions still leave behind inspectable incomplete metadata and surviving artifacts.
- The saved transcript snapshot matches the committed live transcript timeline from the session.
- The saved audio artifacts remain the durable source of record for the session.
- The incremental snapshot strategy means interruption does not require a graceful Stop to preserve transcript state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Stopping a session writes the expected local bundle structure.
- Session metadata includes a timestamp-based default title.
- Interrupted sessions still leave behind incomplete metadata and surviving artifacts.
- The saved transcript snapshot matches the committed live timeline from the session.
- The saved audio artifacts remain the durable source of record.
- Transcript state survives interruption without depending on graceful Stop.
acceptance_criteria: Stopping a session writes the expected local bundle structure. | Interrupted sessions still leave behind incomplete metadata and surviving artifacts. | The saved transcript snapshot matches the committed live timeline from the session.
notes: Validated by spike bd-1yz: persist session metadata early, include the timestamp-based default title, treat per-source raw audio as the durable source of record, and snapshot the committed transcript timeline incrementally so interrupted sessions remain recoverable.
close_reason: Completed: persisted Application Support session bundles with incremental transcript snapshots and incomplete-session recovery state
